title: Fiber-optic based traffic and infrastructure monitoring system
abstract: In an arrangement of a traffic infrastructure and an optic sensor system (10, 20), the traffic infrastructure has a traffic carrying surface (51) for providing support of conveyance elements (92) of a vehicle (90), therewith allowing movements of the vehicle over the traffic carrying surface. The optic sensor system (10, 20) comprising an interrogator (10) and at least one fiber optic sensor (20) arranged below the traffic carrying surface that is coupled to the interrogator. The at least one fiber optic sensor (20) comprising an optic fiber (21) with a plurality of optic strain-sensor elements (22) distributed over its length. The plurality of optic strain-sensor elements (22) have mutually different optical characteristics, e.g. a mutually different characteristic wavelength. The interrogator (10) is configured to transmit an optical interrogation signal into the at least one fiber optic sensor, to receive a response optical signal modulated by said fiber optic sensor, and to identify changes in the optical characteristics of the response optical signal resulting from strains induced in said optic strain-sensor elements as a result of a conveyance element (92) of a vehicle (90) moving over the traffic carrying surface (51) across said at least one fiber optic sensor wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) extends at least substantially according to a straight line in a direction at least substantially parallel to the traffic carrying surface.
claims:
Claims 1. An arrangement (1) of a traffic infrastructure (50) and an optic sensor system (10, 20), the traffic infrastructure having a traffic carrying surface (51) to support conveyance elements (92) of a vehicle (90), therewith allowing movements of the vehicle over the traffic carrying surface, the optic sensor system (10, 20) comprising an interrogator (10) and at least one fiber optic sensor (20) that is coupled to said interrogator and that is arranged below said traffic carrying surface, the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) comprising an optic fiber (21) with a plurality of optic strain-sensor elements (22) distributed over its length, which plurality of optic strain-sensor elements (22) have mutually different optical characteristics, the interrogator (10) being configured to transmit an optical interrogation signal into the at least one fiber optic sensor, to receive a response optical signal that has been modulated by said fiber optic sensor based on its optical characteristics, and to identify changes in the optical characteristics of the response optical signal resulting from strains induced in said optic strain-sensor elements as a result of a conveyance element (92) of a vehicle (90) moving over the traffic carrying surface (51) across said at least one fiber optic sensor, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) extends at least substantially according to a straight line in a direction at least substantially parallel to the traffic carrying surface. 2. The arrangement according to claim 1, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) is provided with at least one anchor element (25) that extends around the at least one fiber optic sensor between mutually subsequent optic strain- sensor elements (22), the at least one anchor element (25) having a circumference in a plane transverse to a longitudinal direction of the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) that is at least 1.5 times larger than a circumference of the fiber optic sensor in a plane transverse to said longitudinal direction at a position of an optic strain-sensor element. 3. The arrangement according to claim 2, wherein said circumference of the at least one anchor element (25) is in a range of 10 to 50 times said circumference of the fiber optic sensor. 4. The arrangement according to claim 2 or 3, wherein the at least one anchor element (25) has a length in the range of 0.1 to 0.7 times a distance between said mutually subsequent optic strain-sensor elements (22). 5. The arrangement according to claim 2, 3 or 4, wherein the at least one anchor element (25) is provided with tangentially extending grooves (251). 6. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein the optic fiber (21) is provided with a non-slip coating (24). 7. The arrangement according to claim 6, wherein the non-slip coating (24) is made of a glass-fiber reinforced polymer (GFRP). 8. The arrangement according to claim 6 or 7, further comprising an intermediate layer (23) arranged between the optic fiber (21) and the non-slip coating (24). 9. The arrangement according to claim 8, wherein the intermediate layer (23) is made of a polyimide. 10. The arrangement according to any of previous claims, wherein said at least one fiber optic sensor (20) is arranged in a direction transverse to a longitudinal direction of the traffic infrastructure (50). 11. The arrangement of claim 10, wherein the traffic infrastructure is a road. 12. The arrangement according to any of claims 1-10 wherein said at least one fiber optic sensor (20) is arranged in a direction longitudinal along the direction of the traffic infrastructure (50) 13. The arrangement of claim 12, wherein the traffic infrastructure is a rail track. 14. The arrangement of claim 13 wherein the arrangement is located below the top surface of the rail track and attached thereto. 15. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims wherein the interrogator is configured to derive a set of signal features indicative for a shape of a temporal pattern of the response optical signal. 16. The arrangement according to claim 15, wherein the interrogator is configured to derive the set of signal features by using a model that relates properties of said conveyance element and or its kinetic state to a shape of the temporal pattern. 17. The arrangement according to claim 15 or 16, wherein the interrogator is configured to compare the temporal pattern of the response optical signal with respective stored temporal patterns and to provide an output signal indicative for the stored temporal pattern that best matches the response optical signal as a signal feature indicative for the shape of the temporal pattern of the response optical signal. 18. The arrangement according to claim 16 or 17, wherein said interrogator is further configured to mutually correlate signal features obtained from a plurality of conveyance elements of a vehicle and to use said mutually correlated signal features to estimate properties of said vehicle. 19. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator is configured to extract a spatial pattern indicative for a spatial footprint of a plurality of tires of a vehicle. 20. The arrangement according to claim 19, wherein the spatial footprint indicates a position of the conveyance elements of the vehicle relative to each other. 21. The arrangement according to claim 19 or 20, wherein the spatial footprint indicates a pressure exerted by the individual conveyance elements of the vehicle. 22. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein respective optical signals of the plurahty of optic strain-sensor elements of the at least one fiber optic sensor are processed to estimate a distance between a pair of tires on a same axle of the vehicle. 23. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein respective optical signals of the plurahty of optic strain-sensor elements of the at least one fiber optic sensor obtained for a first time-interval are processed to correlate those signals with respective optical signals of the plurality of optic strain-sensor elements of the at least one fiber optic sensor in another time interval. 24. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein the optic strain-sensor elements (22) are Fiber Bragg Gratings (FBG). 25. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor (20m) is arranged along a meandering path, the at least one fiber optic sensor (20m) having successive, mutually parallel longitudinal portions that are spaced relative to each other in the traffic direction. 26. The arrangement according to any of claims 1 to 24, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) is one of a plurality of mutually parallel fiber optic sensors (20, 20a, 20n) that are spaced relative to each other in the traffic direction. 27. The arrangement according to any of the preceding claims, wherein the interrogator (10) comprises a tunable laser system to transmit the optical interrogation signal. 28. The arrangement according to claim 27, wherein the tunable laser system varies the wavelength of the optical interrogation signal with a frequency in the range of 0.1 to 5 kHz. 29. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) is arranged in a trench (53) inside a traffic carrying layer (55) defining the traffic carrying surface (51), which trench (53) is filled with a filler material (54). 30. The arrangement according to any of the claims 1 to 28, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) is arranged between a support layer and a traffic carrying layer (55) carried by the support layer. 31. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein the optic sensor system (10,20) is combined with a visual data acquisition system (60) using a data integration module (70), the visual data acquisition system (60) including at least a camera (61) for monitoring traffic on the traffic infrastructure in a filed of view near the at least one fiber optic sensor (20) and an image processing system (62) to provide output data indicative for sensed visual features monitored in said field of view, the data integration module (70) to combine output data provided by said sensor system (10, 20) with output data provided by said visual data acquisition system (60). 32. The arrangement according to any of the previous claims, wherein the optic sensor system (10, 20) is one of a plurality of geographically distributed sensor systems that are communicatively coupled to a central server. 33. The arrangement according to claim 32, wherein the optic sensor system includes a preprocessor (80) to render data preprocessed data from sensed data to be transmitted to said central server. 34. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, further comprising at least one fixed/immovable mounting element at a longitudinal position between mutually opposite sides of the traffic structure. 35. The arrangement according to claim 34, wherein the mounting element extends downward until a depth wherein substantially no deformations occur due to passing by traffic. 36. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the optic fiber further comprises a pair of a first and a second optic strain-sensor element with at least substantially the same optical characteristics in their neutral state, and arranged at mutually opposite sides of a longitudinal section of the at least one fiber optic sensor, and wherein the interrogator is configured to estimate a change in a gap between said first and said second optic strain-sensor element based on an interference between response optic signals received from said first and second optic strain-sensor elements. 37. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, further comprising at least one optic sensor element below said traffic infrastructure surface that is mechanically decoupled from said traffic infrastructure in a longitudinal direction of the fiber optic sensor. 38. The arrangement according to claim 37, wherein the at least one optic sensor element that is mechanically decoupled in a longitudinal direction is mechanically coupled to said infrastructure in a transversal direction. 39. The arrangement according to claim 37, wherein the at least one optic sensor element that is mechanically decoupled in a longitudinal direction is at least substantially mechanically decoupled in any direction, wherein the interrogator module is arranged for determining a temperature based on response optical signals obtained from said mechanically decoupled optic sensor element. 40. The arrangement according to claim 39, wherein the at least one mechanically decoupled optic sensor element is mechanically decoupled from said traffic infrastructure in that it is accommodated in a rigid housing. 41. The arrangement according to claim 40, wherein a longitudinal section of a fiber optic sensor comprising the at least one mechanically decoupled optic sensor element is arranged free from longitudinal tension inside said housing. 42. The arrangement according to claim 41, wherein a longitudinal section of a fiber optic sensor comprising the at least one mechanically decoupled optic sensor element is arranged with a substantially constant pre-strain inside said housing. 43. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein at least one of the optic strain-sensor elements, is mechanically coupled to the traffic infrastructure by a coupling element that selectively inhibits mechanical vibrations having a frequency exceeding a threshold frequency, and wherein the interrogator is configured to interrogate the at least one of the optic strain-sensor elements at a repetition rate of at least twice said threshold frequency. 44. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the plurality of optic strain sensor elements includes a set of strain sensor elements 

 arranged in mutually different angles around a measurement position within a plane substantially parallel to the traffic infrastructure surface, and wherein the interrogator is configured to estimate a strain vector indicative for a magnitude and a direction of a strain at said measurement position. 45. The arrangement according to claim 44, wherein the set of strain sensor elements is part of a single optic fiber. 46. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator is configured to process a response optical signal of an optic strain sensor element to determine a relative position of a conveyance element relative to the optic strain sensor element, using a model that relates the response optical signal to said relative position. 47. The arrangement according to claim 46, wherein the model is based on the presumption that the response optical signal is a symmetric function of the position of the conveyance element relative to the optic strain sensor element. 48. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator is configured to process response optical signals of mutually neighboring optic strain sensor elements to determine a relative position of a conveyance element between the mutually neighboring optic strain sensor elements, using a model that relates a relationship between the response optical signals to said relative position. 49. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator includes a conversion module to convert an optic response signal into a detection signal having a magnitude indicative for a detected strain of the at least one optic strain sensor element and a signal filter to determine high- frequency components in the detection signal, the interrogator being configured to further process the high frequency components to derive signal features related to traffic carried by the traffic carrying surface. 50. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator further includes a signal filter to determine low-frequency components in the detection signal, and to further process the low frequency components to derive signal features related to a mechanical behavior of the traffic infrastructure. 51. The arrangement according to claim 50, wherein the interrogator is configured to decompose the low-frequency components into first and second subcomponents, the first subcomponents being in a lower frequency range than said second subcomponents, wherein the interrogator is configured to further process the first subcomponents to derive signal features related to a mechanical behavior of the traffic infrastructure and to further process the second subcomponents to derive signal features related to a static load of the traffic infrastructure. 52. The arrangement according to one of the claims 49 to 51, wherein the interrogator is configured to identify first and second high frequency subcomponents within said high frequent components in the detection signal, wherein the second high frequency subcomponents are within a frequency range that is higher than a frequency range of said first high frequency components, the interrogator being configured to further process the first high frequency components to derive signal features related to traffic carried by the traffic carrying surface and to further process the second high frequency subcomponents to output an infrastructure condition signal related to a magnitude of said second high frequency subcomponents. 53. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator includes a conversion module to convert an optic response signal into a detection signal having a magnitude indicative for a detected strain of the at least one optic strain sensor element, and wherein the interrogator is configured to determine a change of amplitude in a temporal pattern of the detection signal 

 and to estimate a viscoelastic state of the road using a model that relates said temporal pattern to the viscoelastic state. 54. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, further including at least one acceleration sensor that is mechanically coupled to the traffic infrastructure and a signal processing module that is configured to identify a high frequency component in an output signal provided by the acceleration sensor and to output an infrastructure condition signal related to a magnitude of said high frequency component. 55. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator is configured to process the response optic signal of each optic strain- sensor element in accordance with a specific viscoelastic state of the traffic infrastructure in the neighborhood of said each optic strain-sensor element and to compensate for mutual differences in viscoelastic state in the respective neighborhoods of optic strain-sensor elements. 56. The arrangement according to claim 55, wherein the viscoelastic state in neighborhoods of an optic strain-sensor element is estimated using one or more of the measures of claim 49-53. 57. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator is configured to process the response optic signal of each optic strain- sensor element in accordance with a specific geometrical configuration of the traffic infrastructure in the neighborhood of said each optic strain-sensor element and to compensate for mutual differences in geometrical configuration in the respective neighborhoods of optic strain-sensor elements. 58. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator includes a conversion module to convert an optic response signal into a detection signal having a magnitude indicative for a detected strain of the at least one optic strain sensor element and wherein the interrogator is configured 

 to use information about a relative position of a conveyance element to decompose a response optic signal into a signal component contributed to by that conveyance element and a remainder signal component not contributed to by that conveyance element. 59. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator is configured to use the information about the relative position of the conveyance element to decompose a response optic signal into a signal component contributed to by that conveyance element at a point in time and a remainder signal component not contributed to by that conveyance element at that point in time. 60. The arrangement according to claim 58 or claim 59, wherein information about a relative position of a conveyance element is obtained using the measure specified in claim 46 or 47. 61. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, further comprising at least one further fiber optic sensor, wherein the interrogator is configured to correlate a first response optic signal of the at least one fiber optic sensor with a second response optic signal of the at least one further fiber optic sensor to isolate a common signal pattern within said first and said second optic response signal. 62. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, comprising a plurality of optic strain-sensor elements that are distributed in a longitudinal direction of the road, wherein the interrogator is configured to track motion of a vehicle in said longitudinal direction by associating subsequent response optic signals from respective ones of said optic strain-sensor elements associated with a traversal of said vehicle, to determine a magnitude of these subsequent response optic signals, therewith obtaining a time series of magnitudes and to determine whether a variation is present in said time series. 63. The arrangement according to claim 62, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor is one of a plurality of fiber optic sensors that are arranged at mutually different longitudinal positions transverse to a longitudinal direction of the road, and wherein the plurality of optic strain-sensor elements that are distributed in a longitudinal direction of the road comprise optic strain-sensor elements of respective ones of said plurality of fiber optic sensors. 64. The arrangement according to claim 62, wherein the at least one fiber optic sensor extends in a longitudinal direction of the road. 65. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator includes a signal analysis module for estimating properties of noise present in the response optic signal and a signal filter module to adaptively filter the response optic signal based on the estimation of said properties. 66. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, further comprising one or more optical sensors for sensing objects present on the traffic infrastructure surface and further comprising a data integration module to combine output data from said interrogator and said one or more optical sensors.. 67. The arrangement according to one of the previous claims, wherein the interrogator includes a temperature estimation module to estimate a value for a temperature of an optic strain-sensor element and a compensation module using said estimated value to compensate a response optic signal received from the optic strain-sensor element for temperature.
